phrases.intent_en: Where is the castle hill entrance?
phrases.text_local: Wo ist der Eingang am Burgberg?
phrases.cultural_note: Castle hill (Burgberg) is a common term for elevated historic access points in German-speaking regions; visitors expect a clear landmark cue.

likely_response.translation_en: To the left of the main entrance.

responses.english: To the left of the main entrance.

phrases.intent_en: Can I book the tour at the desk?
phrases.text_local: Kann ich die Führung an der Kasse buchen?
phrases.cultural_note: In German museums, tours are often bookable at the ticket desk; staff will confirm availability.

phrases.intent_en: Is the museum shop past the exit?
phrases.text_local: Liegt der Museumsshop hinter dem Ausgang?
phrases.cultural_note: Shop locations in German museums often require confirmation from staff; avoid assuming placement relative to exits.

likely_response.local: Nein, der Shop ist vor dem Ausgang.
likely_response.translation_en: No, the shop is before the exit.

responses.english: No, the shop is before the exit.
responses.french: Nein, der Shop ist vor dem Ausgang.

phrases.intent_en: Is the next guided tour in English?
phrases.text_local: Gibt es eine Führung auf Englisch gleich?
phrases.cultural_note: Guided tours in German museums often follow fixed schedules; English tours may be less frequent, so staff will specify next time.

phrases.intent_en: Where do I return audio guides?
phrases.text_local: Wo gebe ich die Audioguides zurück?
phrases.cultural_note: Audio guide returns are typically handled at staffed counters in German museums; avoid micro-directions.
